item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.8.0
item.description:
New Features

  • You can now install role-scoped bundles for lead, implementer, critic, designer, and vault personas during bootstrap.
  • You can now generate domain-specific skills focused on repository context for agents working in specialized environments.
  • You can now use CI-instantiated one-core-many-faces templates for consistent project scaffolding across different deployment targets.
  • The harness now includes an eval-coverage gate with seed evaluations for hot-skill assessment and A/B testing framework for skill performance validation.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed bootstrap CLI self-update detection issue that prevented proper version management during installation.
  • Fixed telemetry classification to properly distinguish between direct skill invocations and routed delegations for accurate usage tracking.

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.7.1
item.description:
Bug Fixes
  • Fixed bootstrap CLI self-update mechanism that was failing to detect available updates after initial installation. The bootstrap process now correctly identifies and applies CLI updates during harness maintenance cycles.
]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.7.0
item.description:
New Features

  • You can now generate focused repo-local skills tailored to domain agents using harness-engineering capabilities, automatically creating context-aware skills for specific project domains and workflows.

  • You can now use CI-instantiated project templates through the one-core-many-faces pattern, providing standardized scaffolding for multi-surface applications with consistent architecture.

  • You can now access role-scoped skill bundles for specialized agent types (lead, implementer, critic, designer, vault), allowing targeted capability deployment based on agent responsibilities.

  • The eval system now includes skill evaluation coverage gates and hot-skill evaluation seeding, providing automated quality assurance for agent capabilities with A/B testing framework integration.

]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.6.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now CI-instantiate the one-core-many-faces template, enabling automated template deployment across different harness configurations.
  • You can now use role-scoped bundles for bootstrap (lead/implementer/critic/designer/vault), allowing targeted skill distribution based on agent roles.
  • The telemetry system now classifies skill invocations as direct vs routed, providing better insight into agent execution patterns.
  • You can now wire eval-coverage gates with seeded hot-skill evaluations, establishing automated quality thresholds for skill deployment.
]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.5.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now configure agent harnesses with role-scoped skill bundles (lead, implementer, critic, designer, vault) for targeted capability distribution
  • The skills system now includes an eval-coverage gate that requires evaluation metrics for skill deployment, plus A/B evaluation framework for comparing skill performance
  • You can now generate artifact reports from skill execution to track deliverable quality and completion status
  • The template system now includes baked-in Fly Litestream deployment configuration for streamlined production setup
]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.4.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now gate skill evaluation coverage before deployment. The eval-coverage gate ensures skills have comprehensive test coverage, and hot-skill evaluations are seeded during skill registration for rapid quality feedback.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed telemetry classification to distinguish between direct skill invocations versus routed skill calls. Skill execution tracking now properly categorizes invocation paths for accurate performance monitoring and debugging.
]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.3.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now evaluate skills with the skill-eval A/B framework that includes shape evaluation for testing skill effectiveness and comparison
  • You can now generate artifact reports for skills, providing documentation and analysis of skill outputs and performance
  • You can now deploy with the Fly Litestream layer baked into templates for streamlined database replication and deployment
  • Fixed roster lane dispatch to properly handle bounded routing and prevent dispatch failures
]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.2.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now generate structured reports of agent work artifacts using the new artifact report generator skill
  • You can now deploy projects with baked-in Fly Litestream configuration through the enhanced template system
  • The roster lane dispatch system now includes proper bounds checking to prevent execution errors
  • Fixed bootstrap configuration to correctly distinguish between empty allowlists and missing keys when parsing skill permissions
]]]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.1.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now deploy to Fly.io with built-in Litestream database replication using the template layer
  • You can now bootstrap harnesses with expanded global coverage (13 skills + 4 agents) and skill allowlisting via .spellbook.yaml
  • You can now generate project-specific skills using /harness scaffold mode for local QA automation
  • You can now track agent work sessions and skill invocations through the new trace system with PostToolUse hooks

Improvements

  • The bootstrap process now resolves .spellbook.yaml from git root instead of current directory, fixing path resolution issues
  • The roster dispatch system now properly bounds lane targeting to specific repositories, preventing cross-repo conflicts
  • The CI pipeline now excludes skills/.external from source context and rejects unresolved conflict markers
  • The skill discovery system now scans the filesystem directly instead of relying on registry files for better reliability
]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>

item.title: misty-step/harness-kit v1.0.0
item.description:
New Features
  • You can now use the /deps skill for semantic dependency management with reachability analysis
  • You can now leverage agent-first workflow capabilities including distributed issue tracking via git-bug, Dagger CI integration, and atomic claim protocols
  • You can now scaffold project-local harness configurations using /harness scaffold mode for QA skill generation and demo templates
  • You can now access consolidated global skills (74 skills from 262) with budget-aware selection and multi-provider code review support across Thinktank, Codex, and Gemini

Improvements

  • The bootstrap system now resolves .spellbook.yaml from git toplevel instead of current directory and honors skill allowlists for cleaner installations
  • The focus system now includes second-stage selection heuristics and selection telemetry for better skill targeting across harnesses
  • The tailor system now supports cross-harness installation with canonical .agents/ directories and per-harness symlinks for unified agent management
  • The verdict system now enforces git-native review workflows with persistent scoring and evidence-based quality gates

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed autopilot workflow to create feature branches before spawning builders and stop before merge with LGTM protocol instead of auto-merge
  • Fixed roster lane dispatch scoping to target repositories and bound dispatch execution to prevent incorrect routing
  • Fixed CI gates to exclude skills/.external from source context and reject unresolved conflict markers during validation
  • Fixed skill discovery to use filesystem scanning instead of registry.yaml and properly handle external skill synchronization
]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]]]>]]]]]]>]]]]>]]>
